name: Winterberry Holly
summary: Deciduous wetland holly whose brilliant red berries persist from November through March — feeding cedar waxwings, robins, bluebirds, grosbeaks, and orioles when other winter food is gone. The shrub that sets the December woodland on fire.
notes: Native throughout PA wet hollows, vernal pool edges, and stream banks. Tolerates spring standing water. Larval host for Henry's Elfin. Dioecious — plant one male per ~5 females; named cultivars or open-pollinated bare-root work. Pollinator nectar in late spring. The single highest-impact wetland addition for a property with any wet hollow.

planting.aspect: Wet hollows; stream edges; vernal pool margins
planting.moisture: Wet to moist; tolerates seasonal standing water
planting.site_notes: Site with wet feet — vernal pool edges, swale bottoms, stream margins. Dioecious; mix male and female plants. Wire cage for the first 3 years. Plant in clusters of 5+ to ensure cross-pollination.
